title: Nebula layout: restored the drifting purple/teal background wash
features: Brought back the diagonal background gradient from the original Nebula concept mockup - a purple wash bleeding from the top-left into a teal wash from the bottom-right, slowly drifting over a 22s cycle rather than sitting static. Only Nebula gets this; every other page keeps the smaller, static two-blob background it always had.

title: Nebula layout: account menu repositioned, full nav coverage, richer dashboard
features: Nebula's nav bar now links to every page the sidebar does - Vault, Invitations, Tasks, Settings, and Changelog were missing before, making them unreachable from this layout without knowing the URL directly.

Recent Activity was the only thing filling the bottom of the Dashboard, leaving a lot of empty space below it on desktop. Added Top Viewers (paired next to Recent Activity) and Recent Addons panels, reusing the same data and components Current mode already shows.
bugFixes: Moved the account/profile menu from the topbar's top-right corner to a floating button in the bottom-left, matching where Current mode's own "Administrator" control sits. Its dropdown was also anchored to open leftward (correct for the old top-right position) - now anchors rightward from the bottom-left position instead of clipping off the edge of the screen; added an `align` option to PanelSwitcher to support both.

title: Nebula layout: fixed sparse desktop rendering, added the real logo and an account menu
bugFixes: Nebula's top nav bar and content panels looked sparse and disproportionate at real desktop widths - tiny nav text packed to one side, huge dead space around small stat numbers. Caused by a max-w-6xl Tailwind class that never actually applied: globals.css has an unlayered `* { max-width: 100vw }` rule (added to prevent horizontal overflow) which, per the CSS Cascade Layers spec, always wins over ANY Tailwind utility class regardless of specificity - silently no-oping the width cap. Switched to an inline style, which does win, and confirmed via direct DOM measurement (not just a screenshot) that the bar and content now render at a real, centered 72rem cap on desktop. Also enlarged the nav links, stat card padding/type, and gave the Groups/Addons cards icon badges (matching Current's stat cards) so they aren't just a bare number floating in an oversized box.

The topbar's brand mark was a plain gradient square placeholder - swapped in the actual SlickSync chain-link logo (SlickSyncLogo.tsx) and enlarged the whole brand lockup to match the sidebar's own treatment (gradient-clipped text, larger badge).
miscChores: Nebula's topbar had no way to see who's logged in, switch to the User panel, or log out - the sidebar's equivalent (PanelSwitcher, the "Administrator" control) simply wasn't present. Reused the same PanelSwitcher component as a compact icon button on the topbar's right side rather than rebuilding the menu separately, adding a `variant="compact"` (icon-only trigger) and `dropdownPosition="down"` (sidebar's version opens upward, which would render off-screen at the top of the page) option to it.

title: New Layout option: Nebula, a structural redesign of Dashboard (top nav, glass panels)
features: Settings now has a Layout section, independent of Theme: Theme picks colors, Layout picks structure. The new "Nebula" layout replaces the Dashboard's sidebar with a top nav bar, and restyles its stat cards and panels as translucent glass panels with a gradient top-accent stripe, over the currently-selected color theme's own palette (so it looks right no matter which Theme is active, not just the Nebula color theme it's named after). "Current" (the existing sidebar/card layout) remains available as the other option.

Nebula is the default layout for new sessions, so day-to-day use surfaces any rough edges quickly. Every other page (Users, Groups, Addons, Settings, etc.) keeps its normal sidebar regardless of this setting - only Dashboard has a Nebula-styled render path so far; Activity is next.

title: Rotten Tomatoes and Metacritic ratings, everywhere a poster shows
features: Poster cards on Discover, the Dashboard's Continue Watching row, and Activity's grid view now show Rotten Tomatoes and Metacritic scores alongside IMDb's rating (color-banded green/yellow/red, matching Metacritic's own convention) - previously only a bare IMDb rating showed, and only on Discover. The media detail modal got the same two ratings added next to its existing IMDb/TMDb row. Sourced from OMDb (needs a free OMDb API key set as OMDB_API_KEY - everything degrades gracefully to showing nothing if it's unset, not an error).

New POST /users/ratings-batch endpoint for Discover and Activity's grid views, which render many poster cards at once and can't afford a full metadata round trip per item the way the modal and Continue Watching (which already resolve full metadata per item) do. Callers pass only the deduplicated IMDb IDs actually on screen; results are cached server-side for 7 days per title, so repeat views and overlapping page loads don't re-hit OMDb at all.
Activity's grid gets IMDb ratings for the first time too (not just the two new ones) - its history feed never touched Cinemeta before, so there was no rating data available for it at all until this.

title: More timezone bugs: user self-service "Watch Time Today", binge-watching stats
bugFixes: Extended the earlier timezone sweep (1.10.1) beyond metricsBuilder.js to the rest of the server. Found a second, separate "Watch Time Today" bug on the user self-service page (/user) - its stats endpoint computed "today" as the server process's own local midnight (UTC in Docker) instead of the account's configured timezone, so a real timestamp comparison (not a pre-bucketed WatchActivity.date, which was already correct) silently miscounted for any account west of UTC. Same root cause as the admin Activity page's bug, different code path (server/routes/publicLibrary.js's /activity endpoint) that the first pass didn't cover.

Watch velocity (episodes-per-day / binge-watching stats) bucketed WatchSession.startTime - a real timestamp - by UTC day instead of account day, which could split one binge-watching evening into two counted "days" or merge two separate days into one, skewing the stat.

Confirmed everywhere else that reads WatchActivity.date/WatchSnapshot.date back out (metricsBuilder.js, users.js, groups.js, publicLibrary.js's streak calculation) is already correct as-is - those fields are pre-bucketed to account-day at write time, so extracting the UTC calendar day back out is a lossless round-trip, not a bug, despite looking identical to the buggy pattern at a glance. Also added an explicit timeZone: 'UTC' to one more day-label formatter and a stray, unused-by-the-client startDate/endDate pair on a legacy per-user endpoint, for full consistency.

title: Public mode: Nuvio login for admin accounts, matching the existing Stremio option
features: The admin login page's public-mode toggle now has a third option, "Nuvio Login", alongside "UUID / Pass" and "Stremio Login" - same device-code flow as Stremio's (open the app or scan/enter a code), backed by Nuvio's own OAuth mechanism since it has no single long-lived auth key like Stremio does. Supports both a fresh login (find-or-create an account by the Nuvio account's email) and linking Nuvio to an already-logged-in admin account, with the same conflict handling as the Stremio flow (a Nuvio account already linked elsewhere, or an admin account already linked to a different Nuvio account).

Unlike the Stremio login (which trusts a client-supplied auth key and separately re-validates it), the new /auth/nuvio-login endpoint does the whole OAuth exchange itself server-side and reads the resulting identity straight out of Nuvio's own response - there's no client-supplied email or user ID to spoof at all.

title: Metrics: Watch Time Today could silently undercount, New Signups chart mis-dated late signups
bugFixes: Found while sweeping for the same class of bug as the Changelog date fix. Activity's "Watch Time Today" stat looks up today's entry in the metrics API's watchTime.byDay array by string-matching a locally-computed date - but it used the browser's own local calendar day, while byDay is actually keyed by the account's configured timezone (Settings -> Privacy & Display). Whenever those two disagreed about what day it currently was, the lookup silently found nothing and fell back to 0 base seconds, undercounting until the mismatch resolved itself hours later - plausibly explaining an earlier discrepancy this session where a recently-watched title didn't show up in the total. Added an authoritative today field to the metrics API response (the account-day string the server itself used to build byDay's keys) so the client no longer has to guess.

Separately, the New Signups chart bucketed new users by UTC calendar day instead of the account's configured timezone - a real bug, not just a display issue, since User.createdAt is an actual timestamp: anyone who signed up late in the account's own day (which, for any timezone west of UTC, is a large chunk of every day) had their signup counted on the wrong day's bar.

Also fixed the same UTC-vs-viewer-timezone date-label bug the Changelog page had (added timeZone: 'UTC' consistently) in the three Metrics charts and the Users-page lifecycle card that render account-day bucket strings - Watch Time, Content Breakdown, New Signups (both places it's charted). Confirmed via the write path (metricsProcessor.js) that WatchActivity/WatchSnapshot day-bucketing itself was already correct (pre-computed via getAccountDateString at record time) - only the newly-found spots above were actually broken.

title: Changelog page showed every release one day early
bugFixes: Found while checking that today's own new entries rendered correctly: every release date on this page was off by one calendar day for anyone in a timezone west of UTC - including this project's own documented default, America/Los_Angeles. changelog.json stores dates as plain YYYY-MM-DD strings, which Date parses as UTC midnight, but the page displayed them with toLocaleDateString() and no timeZone option - rendering in the viewer's local time, which sits earlier in the day than UTC midnight west of the prime meridian. Added timeZone: 'UTC' to the formatting call so the displayed date always matches the literal string in the file, regardless of viewer timezone. Affected every entry on the page, not just new ones.

title: Fixed a stuck-state edge case in today's two drag-to-scroll fixes
bugFixes: Self-review of 1.9.97/1.9.98 (deferring setPointerCapture until an actual drag, instead of engaging it on every mouse-down) found a real edge case: capture is what guaranteed the row still received pointerup even if the cursor left its bounds during the drag. Deferring it removed that guarantee for the brief window before the 5px threshold is crossed - a press-and-release that moves mostly vertically off the row (e.g. toward the stat cards above Continue Watching) with under 5px of horizontal travel never fires pointerup on the row at all, leaving its 'pointer is down' state stuck true. The next time the mouse merely hovered over the row afterward (no button held), it would compute scrollLeft from stale drag-start values and yank the row around on its own. Fixed in both Continue Watching (page.tsx) and the media detail modal's Cast row by checking e.buttons on the next move event - if the primary button isn't pressed anymore, the stale state clears itself instead of producing a phantom scroll.

title: Media detail modal: Cast row never actually supported grab-and-drag scrolling
bugFixes: The Cast row in the rich detail modal (opened from Continue Watching's fallback icon, Activity, and Discover) was a plain overflow-x-auto div with no drag-to-scroll of its own - unlike the Dashboard's Continue Watching row, which has always had custom grab-and-drag. Native click-and-drag doesn't scroll a div in any browser by default, only trackpad swipe or shift+scroll-wheel, so mouse users had no way to drag through the cast list. Added the same mouse-only drag-to-scroll Continue Watching has, applying this session's fix directly: pointer capture is deferred until an actual drag crosses the 5px threshold rather than engaged on every mouse-down, so it can't reintroduce the Firefox external-protocol block found earlier today for this same modal's Open in Stremio/Nuvio buttons. Cast photos also got draggable={false} + pointer-events-none, matching the fix already applied to Continue Watching's poster images, so the browser's native image-drag doesn't hijack the gesture.

title: Continue Watching: clicking the card stopped opening Stremio/Nuvio
bugFixes: Clicking a Continue Watching card's poster silently did nothing in Firefox, even with the Stremio/Nuvio desktop app installed and registered - only the fallback detail modal's explicit "Open in Stremio"/"Open in Nuvio" buttons worked. Root cause: the row's drag-to-scroll called setPointerCapture() on every left mouse-down in the row, including a plain click with no drag at all. v1.9.94 already found that pointer capture breaks plain onClick handlers (fixed then by excluding <button> from it) and confirmed ordinary <a href> navigation was unaffected - true for same-origin links, but Firefox specifically distrusts external-protocol handoff (nuvio://, stremio://) when the click's pointer was captured by an ancestor, even though the DOM click event still reaches the anchor normally. Excluding <a> from capture entirely (the obvious fix) was already tried and reverted in v1.9.94 because it broke drag-to-scroll on the poster itself. Fixed properly this time by deferring setPointerCapture until an actual drag is detected (past the existing 5px threshold) instead of engaging it on every mouse-down - a plain click now never touches capture at all, while dragging still works exactly as before.

title: Public/multi-tenant mode: built the missing registration page, fixed a run of real bugs found along the way
features: New self-registration page for public (multi-tenant) mode. The backend's POST /auth/register endpoint already worked, but there was no frontend page or link to reach it at all - a brand-new public-mode deployment was completely unusable through the web UI despite the README documenting a registration screen. Generates a server-side account ID (shown with a copy button and a clear "save this, it can't be recovered" warning, since it doubles as the permanent login identifier), takes a password, and signs straight in on success. Linked from the login page's Admin tab ("Don't have an account? Create one"), public mode only.
bugFixes: The real reason registration couldn't have worked even with a naive implementation: the global auth allowlist only exempted /api/public-auth/register and /api/public-auth/generate-uuid from requiring a valid token, not their /api/auth/... equivalents - the exact paths the rest of the client codebase actually uses for every other auth call (login, stremio-login, etc). Added the missing entries.

POST /auth/register set auth cookies correctly but never returned a token in its JSON response, unlike /auth/login which returns both. Added it, so the client can store it the same way for both flows.

Set up a real local test deployment (Docker + a live Postgres instance) to actually verify public mode end-to-end, since it's never been exercised - INSTANCE=private is all that's ever actually been deployed. Found and fixed three more real gaps this surfaced: migrate deploy (the proper production migration path for Postgres) has likely never worked in this fork's history at all - migration_lock.toml declared provider = "sqlite" while the schema itself is postgresql, so every schema change silently fell back to db push --accept-data-loss instead. Regenerated a correct baseline migration against a real Postgres instance. Also found two fields present in the SQLite schema but missing from the Postgres one - WatchSession's @@unique([accountId, userId, itemId]) (broke the compound-key selector sessionTracker.js and this fork's own duration-persistence fix depend on) and Addon.position (broke the whole Addons page in public mode, added for the drag-and-drop reordering feature well before this session) - both restored.

A CRLF line-ending issue in scripts/start.sh (introduced by this specific Windows checkout's core.autocrlf, not anything actually committed) silently crashed any Docker image built from a Windows machine with "exec: line 8: ./start.sh: not found" - the kernel was looking for a literal "/bin/sh\r" interpreter. Added .gitattributes to force LF for .sh files regardless of the checking-out machine's autocrlf setting, so this can't happen again for anyone else building on Windows.

title: Postgres (public/multi-tenant) schema: WatchSession was missing a constraint the app code depends on
bugFixes: schema.postgres.prisma's WatchSession model was missing the @@unique([accountId, userId, itemId]) constraint that schema.sqlite.prisma has - found while checking whether the SQLite and Postgres schemas had drifted after this session's changes. Prisma only generates the accountId_userId_itemId compound-key selector when the constraint actually exists in the schema, and sessionTracker.js's upsert-based session tracking (plus this session's watch-history duration persistence fix) both depend on exactly that selector - a genuine gap for anyone running public/multi-tenant mode, not just a cosmetic difference. Added the missing constraint to match SQLite. A fresh public-mode deploy applies this automatically via db push on next boot; an existing public deployment with real data would only hit an issue if duplicate (accountId, userId, itemId) rows already exist in watch_sessions, since a unique constraint can't be added over existing duplicates.

title: Discover: browse & search catalogs, open in Stremio/Nuvio + Continue Watching interaction fixes
features: New "Discover" page (sidebar, under Overview): browse Cinemeta's real catalogs - Popular, New, and Top Rated - for movies and series, or search by title. This is a different Cinemeta host than the meta-only one already used elsewhere (that one's own manifest declares zero catalogs); v3-cinemeta.strem.io is the one that actually exposes browse/search, confirmed by reading its manifest directly rather than assumed. Search only works against the "Popular" catalog, since that's the only one the manifest advertises search support for. Click any poster to open the same rich detail modal used elsewhere.

That modal now has "Open in Stremio" / "Open in Nuvio" buttons, usable everywhere it appears (Activity, Continue Watching's fallback icon, and Discover) - not a Discover-only feature. Colored to match each provider's own identity badge (purple for Stremio, blue/orange for Nuvio) rather than a generic accent color.

The Stremio/Nuvio deep-link builders are now shared between Continue Watching and Discover in one module instead of duplicated. Stremio's movie and no-specific-episode link formats came from Stremio's own SDK documentation (movies use videoId = id; a series with no chosen episode omits videoId entirely to open its episode list) rather than guessed at.
bugFixes: The Continue Watching info-icon button did nothing at all - not about what it opened (IMDb, then the detail modal, neither worked), but that the row's own drag-to-scroll called setPointerCapture() on any left-click anywhere in the row, and per the Pointer Events spec, once an element captures a pointer, every subsequent click for that pointer gets redirected to the capturing element instead of whatever's actually under the cursor. The button's click handler never received the event. The card's own link kept working the whole time because native browser navigation isn't affected by that same JS-level redirection - only plain onClick handlers are.

Fixing that required excluding <button> presses from engaging the row's drag-capture - but the first attempt also excluded <a> presses, which broke drag-to-scroll on the poster itself (most of each card's surface), since the card's link never actually had the pointer-capture problem. Narrowed back to just <button>.

Right-clicking a different Continue Watching card while another card's menu was open left the old one orphaned on screen. Each card was tracking its own context-menu state independently; a second card's own stopPropagation() (needed to suppress the browser's native right-click menu) cut off the event before the first card's "close on click elsewhere" listener could fire. Fixed by lifting "which card's menu is open" to one shared value instead of one per card, so opening a new one closes any other by construction.
Every release's title on this Changelog page was invisible - smuggled into an internal section that only rendered when it had list items, and a title has none. Now shown under the version number.

The "miscChores" field was parsed from this file but never rendered anywhere - any release with only chores and no features/bug fixes expanded to a completely empty card. Added a proper "Chores" section.
The footer's GitHub link pointed at the upstream Syncio author's account instead of this repo. Fixed, and each version number now links to that specific release's page.

title: Nuvio deep links confirmed via source, watch history persistence, Continue Watching hardening
features: Continue Watching now opens the Nuvio app directly for Nuvio accounts. Rather than guess at a URL format, read NuvioMedia/NuvioDesktop's own source (AppUrlBridge.kt) directly - it registers nuvio:// on Windows (Desktop 0.1.11-alpha+) and its own deep-link parser accepts nuvio://meta?type=series&id={imdbId}, built from the app's own link-builder function. One real limitation: unlike Stremio's link, Nuvio's format has no season/episode parameter at all, so it opens the show's own page rather than jumping straight to the next episode.

A small always-functional fallback link is now shown on every Continue Watching card with an app link, since a page has no way to detect whether a custom-scheme click actually found an app to catch it. Opens the web version directly - IMDb for Nuvio, web.stremio.com for Stremio.
bugFixes: MovieWatchHistory/EpisodeWatchHistory rows never actually stored a duration. The field always stayed null - what looked like a working duration on the Activity page was a separate in-memory merge computed fresh on every page load, never persisted to the row itself. The native pipeline now writes the real duration at create/update time, using the same "only ever increases" merge rule already used everywhere else duration gets reconciled in this codebase. Added scripts/backfill-history-durations.js to fix already-written rows retroactively (dry-run by default, --apply to write).

Right-click remove on Continue Watching had a real bug: the row's mouse-drag handler didn't check which button was pressed, so a right-click also engaged the drag-scroll logic and captured the pointer. Scoped to the left mouse button only, and the remove action now confirms with a toast.
The fallback icon on each card now opens the real detail modal (cast, trailer, rating, IMDb/TMDb links) instead of a bare external link - the same modal the Activity page's poster click already uses.

title: Continue Watching & detail modal polish, mobile performance
bugFixes: Continue Watching links to Nuvio opened nothing. Switching both providers onto the same stremio:// deep link removed Nuvio's plain web fallback - a browser silently does nothing when navigating to a custom scheme with no registered handler. An attempt at a JS-driven fallback (try the app link, fall back to web after a timeout) turned out fragile itself - a popup-blocker false positive, and worse, it regressed the Stremio link that was already working. Browsers handle a direct anchor click far more reliably than script-driven navigation for custom URL schemes, even from inside a real click handler. Reverted to a plain native <a href> with no JS interception at all.

Nuvio's app link never had anywhere to go on this test setup - its desktop client is alpha/testers-only with no confirmed protocol-handler registration, so no scheme was ever going to be caught there. Fell back to IMDb's title page instead - provider-neutral and guaranteed to work - rather than guessing at another unverified link format.

Drag-to-scroll mostly grabbed the poster image instead of scrolling the row, showing the browser's "can't drop here" cursor - links and images are draggable by default, hijacking the custom scroll gesture. Disabled native drag explicitly.

Cast names were truncating almost immediately, and the detail modal's text overall was too small to read comfortably. Widened cast cards, switched to two-line wrap instead of single-line truncation, and sized up title/body/metadata text across the whole modal.

The trailer video looked squished after the modal was widened - its container had a fixed short height regardless of the new width, and YouTube's player always preserves 16:9 internally, so it letterboxed into a thin strip. Now derives height from width instead.
The trailer's close button now returns to the poster/details view instead of closing the whole modal, and was repositioned clear of YouTube's own top-right settings/CC/volume controls.

Opening the cast/details modal stuttered hard on phones. The shared modal's dimming overlay ran a full-viewport backdrop-filter blur, recomputed every frame of the open/close animation - a well-known jank source on phone GPUs. The blur now only applies at tablet/desktop widths; the plain dimming reads fine on its own.

Cast photos and Continue Watching thumbnails now lazy-load with async decoding instead of decoding eagerly on mount, which was competing with the modal's animation and the metadata fetch for the same main thread.

Continue Watching's drag-to-scroll was capturing every pointer type, fighting the page's native vertical scroll on a phone. Scoped to mouse only - touch and pen get native horizontal scrolling, which also has proper momentum the custom version lacked.
The media-details endpoint was shipping a show's entire episode list (hundreds of entries for long-running series) even though the modal only displays the current one. Stripped from that response.
Added preconnect hints for the two image CDNs the modal's images load from, and trimmed the open/close animation from 300ms/200ms to 200ms/150ms.

title: Media detail modal with trailers, Continue Watching, and a run of real watch-tracking data bugs fixed
features: Click any poster on the Activity page to see cast (with photos), IMDb rating, genres, director, runtime, and awards - all pulled from Cinemeta, the same free service already used for posters, so no new API key or account to manage.
Trailers play inline in the detail modal via an embedded YouTube player instead of linking out to YouTube.

New "Continue Watching" row on the Dashboard: the next unwatched episode for any show someone's partway through, most recently watched first. Click-and-drag to scroll the row (Pointer Events, works with mouse, touch, or pen), right-click a card to remove it from the row.

Continue Watching links open straight into the Stremio or Nuvio app on the next episode - confirmed against Nuvio's own Android source that it registers to handle Stremio-format deep links too, not just its own.

Cinemeta metadata lookups are now cached server-side for 24h, so reopening the same title's detail modal (or two household members opening the same one) is instant instead of a fresh external round trip every time.
bugFixes: Activity feed's per-item duration merge was matching sessions to history entries using stale title/timestamp logic left over from before the proxy pipeline stopped writing watch sessions - switched to matching by real item ID, which is what actually fixed "Yesterday" and other days permanently stuck at 0h.

A session's duration now seeds from timeOffset (a position bounded by the item's own runtime) instead of always starting at 0 - fixes any single-sitting, no-pause watch, which previously could never get a duration no matter how long it was, since the position-delta calculation needs two checkpoints and a straight-through watch only ever produces one.

"Watch Time Today" / Watch Time Trend could be inflated by a real data race in Nuvio's multi-profile sync: a transient per-profile fetch failure could let a different, long-stale profile reading briefly resurface as if it were new progress. The delta calculation now compares against the highest value ever recorded for an item, not just the immediately prior one, so a revert-then-recover can never register as new watch time again.

Logging in via Stremio OAuth (or accepting a Stremio-provider invite) could resolve to the wrong account when the same email had both a Stremio and a Nuvio user record on file - now correctly scoped to the right provider.

The User self-service panel (/user/*) had no mobile drawer at all - the sidebar was a fixed 240px column with no responsive handling, silently clipping content on any phone. Ported the Admin panel's working drawer pattern over. Also fixed: modals could push their buttons off-screen on short viewports, several icon-only action rows were invisible on touch (hover-only), and a handful of cramped two-field form rows now stack on narrow screens instead of squeezing.
Settings' Default View Mode control silently disappeared on mobile with no explanation (list view genuinely isn't usable at phone width) - now shows a short note instead of a blank gap.

Detail modal: cast members were rendering as "[object Object]" (Cinemeta's richer cast field returns objects, not plain names), the modal had no way to close it at all in some cases, and the trailer's close button sat directly on top of YouTube's own settings/fullscreen controls.
miscChores: Removed release-please and semantic-release configs - neither was ever wired to a workflow; releases are cut by pushing a version tag.

Added several read-only diagnostic scripts (debug-history-duration-merge, debug-watch-activity-today, debug-item-timeline) and dry-run/--apply backfill scripts (fix-zero-duration-sessions, fix-cross-provider-auth-key, reconcile-stale-snapshot-spikes) used to find and verify the fixes above on real production data.
Documented the timeOffset-vs-overallTimeWatched distinction and the running-max delta fix in CLAUDE.md so a future session doesn't regress either one.

title: Watch tracking, settled: two pipelines with one job each - plus strict posters, usenet History, and timezone-aware days
features: Now Playing and History are now fed by two deliberately separate pipelines. The AIOStreams proxy owns live presence (it sees streams open and close in real time); each provider's own library polling owns the record - History, duration, Watch Time - for every source including ones the proxy never carries. Neither can do the other's job, and the split is now enforced rather than assumed.

Correct-or-nothing posters. Proxy-detected entries, where the only clue is a title parsed out of a filename, go through a strict Cinemeta search that returns a poster only on an exact normalized-title match (plus an exact year match when the filename carries a year). No confident match means no poster, rather than a confidently wrong one.

Explicit account timezone (Settings -> Privacy & Display). Background pollers have no request context to infer a viewer's timezone from, so the day boundary for Watch Time Today and streaks is now a setting instead of an accident of UTC. Defaults to the ACCOUNT_TIMEZONE env var.
The 'started watching' notification now fires from the proxy the instant a stream opens. It previously fired at stop time, which read as an end-of-watch notification.
Drag-and-drop reordering on the Addons page (grid view), matching Vault's pattern. Order persists server-side via a new position field instead of resetting on refresh.
Request-count detail on Activity cards, matching AIOStreams' own Proxy History tab.

Vault notification settings moved into Settings -> Notifications, alongside per-type toggles for activity, sync, and invites. Vault alerts ride the same webhook as everything else - there's no separate Vault notification config to keep in sync anymore.
Vault: dropped the 'All' tab in favour of defaulting to the first real category, and AIOStreams entries now show their UUID label rather than a Dashboard URL.
bugFixes: Now Playing no longer shows an already-exited stream as playing for ~15 minutes. The provider only checkpoints at pause/stop and then holds a session 'active', so presence is now proxy-driven whenever AIOStreams is configured.
Usenet playback appears in Now Playing, History, and Watch Time again. It never touches the proxy, so it needs native tracking - and the proxy's reconciliation was blanking it out.
Proxy/native reconciliation is per-title, not per-user. Suppressing per-user meant that finishing one proxied stream would blank out unrelated usenet playback for the same person.

The proxy no longer writes watch history or durations. A proxy connection's wall-clock lifetime is not watch time - AIOStreams holds stale connections open for hours, which once recorded a 5-minute view as 22 hours and, accumulated across replays, inflated Watch Time Today to 54 hours.
Duration merges between the two pipelines take max(), not sum(). Both observe the same minutes, so summing double-counted every watch seen by both.
Watch Time Today bucketed by UTC rather than the configured local timezone, so the day rolled over at the wrong hour.
A first-time watch recorded the account's entire cumulative total as a single day's delta.
Recording a duration delta and advancing the snapshot baseline now happen in one transaction. A restart landing between them made the next poll re-record the identical delta.
Sync status no longer reports false-unsynced: addon comparison is order-insensitive, so the same set in a different order matches.
Live addon count uses a provider-agnostic lookup instead of a Stremio-only fetch, so it's correct for Nuvio users.
'Watching for X' now shows on proxy Now Playing entries of any length, not just long ones.
Deduplication handles bare episode markers (E01 without an S01 prefix) and duplicates within a single session list, not just across pipelines.
Tasks export selectors show the correct Nuvio/Stremio label and the user's real avatar.
Activity and snapshot writes are atomic, with a grace period before treating a proxy connection as closed.
The scripts/ directory is copied into the production image, not just start.sh.
miscChores: Removed the AIOMetadata poster-lookup integration entirely. Its fuzzy catalog matching routinely picked the wrong same-titled film; the strict Cinemeta search above replaces it.
Removed the dead usenet history writer and a set of one-off patch scripts.
Documented the watch-tracking architecture in CLAUDE.md, since this split has been reintroduced-as-a-bug more than once.
Added regression tests over the most-patched-over-time logic, and fixed the test runner.
Removed unused release-please and semantic-release configs - neither tool was wired to a workflow. Releases are cut by pushing a version tag.

title: Now Playing: AIOStreams proxy integration
features: Added a new Now Playing data source backed by AIOStreams' built-in stream proxy, which sees every active stream in real time regardless of provider (Stremio or Nuvio). Stored in a new, independent ProxyStreamSession table - deliberately not merged into the existing WatchSession table, which is a separate, currently-unreliable pipeline.

title: Nebula default theme (redone - earlier attempt never actually landed) + accurate watch duration everywhere
features: Nebula is now the default theme - the previous v1.9.37 tag was found to point at the wrong commit (identical to v1.9.36), meaning this change never actually shipped despite appearing to. Redone properly this time: ThemeContext default, useState initial value, localStorage fallback, and the CSS :root selector all changed from slick to nebula.
bugFixes: Live 'Watching for Xm' display and permanently-stored durationSeconds (written when a session closes) both now compute from real watched-content position delta instead of wall-clock/checkpoint-timestamp math, which could understate or fully zero out real watch time whenever the provider's checkpoint went a while without updating.

Added lastPosition and totalDuration fields to WatchSession (nullable, captured at every create/update/close) - a direct position/runtime snapshot rather than a derived elapsed-time calculation, inspired by reviewing Sonicx161/AIOManager's approach. Structurally more robust against sparse provider checkpoints since it's always accurate the instant new data arrives.

title: Fix two real issues found tonight: activity threshold clarity, and inaccurate 'Watching for' duration
bugFixes: 'Watching for Xm' on Now Playing cards was computed purely client-side as (now - session start time), ticking up every second regardless of whether the provider's actual playback position had moved at all - confirmed with a real case where the position stayed frozen at the same value for 8+ minutes while the displayed counter kept climbing. Added a startPosition field to WatchSession, captured when a session starts; durationSeconds on the backend now reflects real watched-content progress (position delta since the session began), refreshed once per poll cycle, and the frontend now displays that value directly instead of an independent wall-clock tick that overstated progress whenever the provider went a while between checkpoints.
miscChores: Confirmed (not a bug): a title appearing in the live 'Now Playing' banner but not yet in the 'Today' history grid is correct, intentional behavior - Now Playing only needs any nonzero progress, while the history grid requires 5% of runtime (the threshold added earlier tonight to filter out library-preview hovers). Two different, deliberately different bars for two different questions ('is something playing right now' vs 'did they actually watch this').

title: Now Playing: shorter lingering after a real stop
bugFixes: Confirmed via heartbeat trace: 20 minutes (v1.9.32) was too long a grace period - a session sat showing 'Live' with ageMinutes climbing 1 through 8+ with zero new checkpoints, just coasting on the last real ping before the user stopped. Reduced the freshness window to 15 minutes (3x poll interval), just above the measured 13.4-minute worst-case checkpoint gap that justified widening it in the first place - and now consistent with the separate hasRecentActivity check elsewhere in the same file, which already used this same 3x window. There's inherent tension here (Nuvio's data has no 'just stopped' signal, only 'last saved position'), so this is a judgment call, not a guaranteed-correct number - may need revisiting if wider checkpoint gaps are ever measured again.

title: The actual root cause: any repeat watch of anything could never create a session, ever
bugFixes: Found via the v1.9.33 pipeline tracing: WatchSession's schema has @@unique([accountId, userId, itemId]) - one row per item for the account's entire lifetime, meant to be reused/reactivated across repeat watches. sessionTracker.js was calling a plain create() every time no in-memory active session existed for an item, which threw 'Unique constraint failed' on every single replay of anything ever watched before - and that error was being silently swallowed as an assumed-harmless race-condition guard. In practice this meant only an item's very first-ever watch could produce a session; every watch after that, on any day, silently produced nothing. This is the actual explanation for 'Now Playing' and 'Top Watched' both being wrong all session - not the freshness window, not the scheduler, not Nuvio's checkpoint cadence, though those were real secondary issues fixed along the way. Replaced create() with upsert() keyed on the same unique constraint, so a repeat watch reactivates the existing row (fresh startTime, isActive, reset duration) instead of failing to insert a duplicate.

title: Trace the full pipeline through actual session creation/update
bugFixes: A checkpoint confirmed fresh (~5min old, well within the new 20min window) still didn't produce a visible session. Added heartbeats through the rest of the pipeline past the gate check: whenever an item is detected active, and at the actual prisma.watchSession.create/update calls (success and failure), to catch a possible silent DB-level failure (e.g. a unique constraint issue) that the gate-level tracing wouldn't reveal.

title: Real fix for Now Playing: freshness window was proven too tight
bugFixes: Root-caused via live heartbeat tracing during an active watch session: isActivelyWatching()'s 7.5-minute freshness window (v1.9.26) was too tight for Nuvio's actual checkpoint cadence - direct evidence showed a real 13.4-minute gap between two checkpoints during genuinely continuous playback, meaning even the widened v1.9.26 window still produced false negatives. A separate check in the same file (hasRecentActivity, using a 15-minute window) correctly evaluated the exact same data as 'recent', but wasn't the check actually gating session creation. Widened isActivelyWatching's window to 20 minutes (4x poll interval), matching and slightly exceeding the already-correct hasRecentActivity window. Removed the item-specific hardcoded tracing added for this investigation, kept the general heartbeat infrastructure (proven broadly useful) in place.

title: Trace the library fetch and gate evaluation for the specific stuck item
bugFixes: v1.9.30 confirmed the scheduler runs healthily every minute, but a fresh Nuvio checkpoint (5 minutes old, well within the freshness window) still wasn't reaching WatchSession despite several complete cycles running after it appeared. Added heartbeat instrumentation at the one remaining gap: whether getLibraryForUser's live Nuvio fetch actually succeeds (vs a silently-caught failure falling back to stale cache), whether the specific item makes it into the merged library, and how it evaluates against sessionTracker's hasWatchProgress/isActivelyWatching gates - tracing the exact point where a genuinely fresh signal stops producing a session.

title: File-based heartbeat diagnostics for the activity monitor scheduler
bugFixes: console.log output from the backend process has not reliably reached docker logs during steady-state operation - confirmed via a completely empty unfiltered log dump over a 20-minute window despite active traffic and DB evidence of the scheduler not running (WatchSession table 13+ hours stale despite fresh upstream data). This is the same class of issue v1.9.2's stdbuf fix targeted but never confirmed working for bun specifically. Added direct file-based heartbeat logging (bypassing stdout/stderr entirely, same proven approach as the Stremio Vault diagnostics) at every checkpoint in the activity monitor scheduler: server/index.js's call site, scheduleActivityMonitor's init, checkAllAccounts, and every stage of checkActivityForAccount including account lookup, user lookup, metrics processing, and session processing. Retrieve with: docker exec slicksync cat /app/data/activity-monitor-debug.log

title: Much more detailed README
miscChores: Expanded README.md with a full feature-by-feature breakdown (Multi-Provider Sync, Activity Tracking & Now Playing, Vault, Addon Snapshots, Themes with all 10 listed, Metrics & Dashboard, Security Hardening) instead of terse bullet summaries - documents what SlickSync actually does in the kind of detail a new reader would need.

title: Remove unused release-please CI workflow
miscChores: Deleted .github/workflows/release-please.yml - it required a RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN repo secret that was never configured, so it failed on every push. This project's release process has been manual (git tag + gh release create) throughout, which already works fine and doesn't need this automation.

title: Poll cycle down to 1 minute (from 5)
features: The main poll cycle (activityMonitor's scheduled check - library sync, watch history, session tracking, Discord notifications) now runs every 1 minute instead of every 5. sessionTracker's freshness window (how long a lastWatched timestamp counts as 'still watching') is a separate, independent constant and was deliberately NOT shortened to match - it stays at its current generous window so the v1.9.26 fix isn't undone. Worth knowing: this means 5x more requests to Nuvio/Stremio's backend per account/profile - should be fine at typical account counts, but is worth reconsidering first if login/sync errors start appearing that didn't before.

title: Fix Now Playing never detecting genuine active viewing
bugFixes: isActivelyWatching() required the watch-state hash (lastWatched/position) to change between two consecutive 5-minute polls before counting an item as 'still being watched', falling back to a time-based check only on the very first observation after a server restart. That assumed the provider updates lastWatched continuously during playback - but Nuvio only writes a checkpoint on pause/stop/background (established earlier this session), so during genuine continuous viewing the hash frequently doesn't change between polls at all, and the comparison concluded 'user stopped' on every poll after the first - a false negative that could make 'Now Playing' never appear for an entire active session. Now relies on time-based freshness alone (lastWatched within 7.5 minutes), trading a small staleness tail after playback genuinely stops for reliably detecting that it's actually happening.

title: Get real ground-truth on the Stremio Vault check by bypassing the wrapper entirely
bugFixes: v1.9.24 fixed a real bug (the authResult truthiness requirement), but the follow-up debug log showed store.authKey was also never set on any attempt - meaning either the login was genuinely failing, or the store.login() wrapper's side-effect-based signaling has another layer of ambiguity. checkStremioAuth now bypasses store.login() entirely and calls store.request('login', {email, password}) directly, logging Stremio's exact raw API response to /app/data/vault-debug.log and checking result.authKey directly - ground truth, no wrapper or side-effect inference involved.

title: Fix Stremio Vault check: it could never report success, for any credentials
bugFixes: The Stremio credential check (checkStremioAuth) required both authResult to be truthy AND store.authKey/tempStorage.auth to be set before reporting success. Traced via the new vault-debug.log diagnostics: stremio-api-client's store.login() always resolves to undefined by design, on success and failure alike - its internal .then() callback has no return statement, and success is signaled entirely through the side-effect of setting store.authKey via userChange(), not through the resolved promise value. Requiring authResult to also be truthy made the success branch permanently unreachable, for any credentials, valid or not - this was never actually a wrong-password or rate-limiting issue. Real API errors (genuinely wrong passwords, etc.) already threw correctly and were unaffected. Now checks store.authKey/tempStorage.auth alone.

title: Stremio Vault diagnostics: write to a file instead of stdout/stderr
bugFixes: v1.9.22's console.warn diagnostic logging for the Stremio Vault check never showed up in `docker logs` at all. The v1.9.2 stdbuf line-buffering fix for this backend process was applied but its own changelog admitted it was never actually confirmed to work with bun's I/O internals, which may not route through the glibc buffered-stdio calls stdbuf intercepts. Switched to writing diagnostics directly to /app/data/vault-debug.log via a synchronous file write instead, bypassing stdout/stderr entirely - retrieve with `docker exec slicksync cat /app/data/vault-debug.log`.

title: Fix version badge drift for good, deeper Stremio Vault diagnostics
bugFixes: The version badge got stuck on v1.9.9 again - .release-please-manifest.json was fixed once several patches ago but not kept in sync afterward, since every patch since only bumped package.json. Flipped next.config.ts's priority order to check package.json first (the one file reliably updated every release) and only fall back to the manifest/server-version files if it's missing - this should prevent the badge from silently drifting stale again even if the manifest isn't touched.

The v1.9.21 Stremio Vault check improvement didn't surface new detail on the next test (authResult had neither a thrown exception nor an .error/.message field) - added raw server-side logging (docker logs) of the full auth result/store state so the actual response shape is visible on the next failed check, since the Vault card itself can only show a short string.

title: Lighter Nuvio badge orange, Lagoon replaced with Nebula theme, better Stremio Vault check diagnostics
features: Nuvio badge's orange half is now more transparent (opacity 0.22 -> 0.10), less visually heavy against the blue half.
Replaced the Lagoon theme with Nebula: same dark blue-black structure as Midnight, but with the violet-to-cyan accent gradient from Slick instead of amber.
bugFixes: Vault's Stremio credential check (checkStremioAuth) returned a generic 'Stremio rejected these credentials' with no further detail on failure, making it impossible to tell a genuinely wrong password apart from rate-limiting or other transient API issues. Now surfaces whatever detail Stremio's API actually returned, and specifically flags rate-limit responses as 'try again later, not necessarily a bad password' rather than lumping every failure into one generic message.

title: Two-tone Nuvio badge (blue + orange)
features: Nuvio provider badge is now a split two-tone pill - half the existing blue, half a light transparent orange - to stand apart from Stremio's solid purple badge at a glance. Stremio badge unchanged.

title: Larger sidebar logo/text, darker Slick theme background
features: Sidebar logo badge, mark, and 'SlickSync' wordmark are larger (badge 40px -> 56px, mark 28px -> 40px, text text-lg -> text-2xl), plus nav item labels and section headers (OVERVIEW/MANAGEMENT/SYSTEM) bumped up a size. Scoped to the sidebar only - main content areas unchanged.

Slick theme's background/surface colors darkened toward near-black (bg #0f0a1a -> #050308, surface #1a1128 -> #0c0812). Primary/secondary accent colors, text colors, and every other theme are unchanged.

title: Softer Slick theme, two new themes (Velvet, Lagoon), fixed provider badge colors
features: Softened the Slick theme's palette - the original vivid violet (#a855f7) + electric cyan (#22d3ee) combination read as harsh/neon. Replaced with a lower-saturation periwinkle-violet (#8b7ec8) + muted teal (#5fd4c4).

Added two new themes: Velvet (deep plum with dusty rose-mauve + warm gold-tan accents) and Lagoon (deep teal with calm coastal blue accents), both following the same structure as the existing 8 themes and appearing automatically in the Settings theme picker.

Stremio and Nuvio provider badges (on the Users list and user detail page) now use fixed colors - light purple/transparent for Stremio, light dark-blue/transparent for Nuvio - independent of the active theme. Previously they used --color-primary/--color-secondary, which end up nearly identical on themes like Ember, making the two provider badges indistinguishable from each other.

title: Fix missing icons on imported Nuvio addons
bugFixes: Nuvio-imported addons showed a generic puzzle-piece icon instead of the addon's real logo, while Stremio-imported addons showed real icons correctly. Cause: Nuvio's own addon storage is minimal ("urlOnly" sync mode - just {url, name} per addon, no logo/version/description), unlike Stremio's account API which returns full manifests directly. The v1.9.11 fix used Nuvio's minimal data as-is. Now fetches each addon's own manifest.json (5s timeout, best-effort per addon so one dead addon server doesn't fail the whole import) to get the real logo, version, and description - same data a user would see in Nuvio/Stremio itself. Note: addons already imported before this fix won't retroactively gain an icon - re-import/reconnect them to pick up real icon data.

title: Final logo: chain-link mark (reverted from double-S)
bugFixes: The double-S mark from v1.9.15 read fine in a full-size mockup but collapsed into an unreadable blur at the actual ~28px sidebar icon size - the letterform's fine internal negative space doesn't survive that much downscaling. Reverted to the simpler chain-link mark (two solid rounded hooks, no fine internal detail), which stays legible at icon size. Same theme-reactive approach either way: flat white fill inside the sidebar's theme-colored badge.

title: Final logo: tilted interlocked double-S mark
features: Replaced the chain-link mark from v1.9.14 with a tilted, tightly interlocked double-S design - two copies of the real Orbitron Black 'S' glyph (extracted directly from the font file, not live text, so it renders identically regardless of what fonts are installed), overlapped so the strokes actually cross rather than just touching, tilted ~18 degrees for motion. Same theme-reactive approach as before: flat white fill inside the sidebar's theme-colored badge, so it adapts across all 8 themes automatically. Updated everywhere SlickSyncLogo renders, plus regenerated favicon/apple-touch-icon on the brand's Slick violet-cyan gradient.

title: New chain-link logo, now theme-reactive
features: Replaced the gradient spark mark from v1.9.13 (which clashed with the sidebar's own theme-colored badge background) with a chain-link icon representing 'sync', built as an inline SVG component (SlickSyncLogo) instead of a static image. It now automatically adapts to whichever of the 8 themes is active, since the surrounding badge already uses --color-primary/--color-secondary and the mark itself is flat white - checked for contrast against every theme's palette. Applied everywhere the logo renders in-app: sidebar (admin and user), login page, invite pages, and the create/reconnect user modal. Favicon and apple-touch-icon remain static files (browser tab icons can't be theme-reactive) using the same chain-link mark on the brand's Slick violet-cyan gradient.

title: New custom logo, distinct from the original SlickSync branding
features: Replaced the plain circular 'S' badge (inherited from upstream SlickSync) with an original angular spark mark in a violet-to-cyan gradient, matching the app's existing Slick theme palette. Updated everywhere the old logo appeared: sidebar (admin and user), invite pages, the create-user modal, favicon, and apple-touch-icon.

title: Fix 'vunknown' badge on imported Nuvio addons
bugFixes: The v1.9.11 fix defaulted an addon's version to the string 'unknown' when Nuvio's addon data didn't include one (Nuvio's addon rows have no version field at all). The frontend's VersionBadge only skips rendering when version is falsy, so it rendered the literal string 'unknown' with a 'v' prefix - showing 'vunknown' on every imported Nuvio addon. Changed the fallback to null so the badge correctly doesn't render at all when no version is known, same as it already does for other addons missing version info.

title: Fix addon import for non-Stremio accounts (e.g. Nuvio)
bugFixes: The Import button's underlying GET /:id/stremio-addons route was hardcoded to Stremio's API regardless of the user's actual providerType, so any Nuvio (or other non-Stremio) account always failed with 'User is not connected to Stremio' - even though Nuvio has its own working getAddons() provider method already used elsewhere (e.g. the live addon count on the Users list). The route now checks providerType and uses the generic provider.getAddons() for non-Stremio accounts, transforming Nuvio's minimal addon shape into the same response shape the frontend already expects.

title: Fix the last two avatar gaps: live sessions and the Now Playing banner
bugFixes: v1.9.6/v1.9.7 threaded avatarUrl through recentActivity and watchActivity.byUser, but two more sources build their own user objects independently: nowPlaying (used by the Dashboard's Recent Activity panel and the Activity page's Now Playing banner) and watchSessions (completed DB sessions with real tracked duration - e.g. the Obsession card with its '34s' badge). Both were still falling back to generic initials even for accounts with a real avatar set, which is why the same account could show two different-looking avatars across cards on the same page. All four avatar sources (recentActivity, watchActivity.byUser, nowPlaying, watchSessions) now carry avatarUrl/useGravatar consistently.

title: Fix version badge stuck at v1.9.2
bugFixes: The sidebar version badge and Changelog page's 'Current' marker read .release-please-manifest.json first, before falling back to package.json. None of the v1.9.3-v1.9.8 patches updated that file, so the app kept reporting v1.9.2 as current even though newer code was actually running. Updated the manifest to match.

title: Fix the real cause of bookmarked-but-unwatched titles reappearing every poll cycle
bugFixes: The isActuallyWatched() fixes in v1.9.3 and v1.9.6 were never actually the gatekeeper for this - they only affected Discord notifications and a legacy library-scan heuristic. The code path that actually writes to MovieWatchHistory/EpisodeWatchHistory (processLibraryItem -> recordMovieWatch/recordEpisodeWatch in metricsProcessor.js) had no 'was this actually watched' check at all - recordMovieWatch's only gate was item.type === 'movie'. A pure library bookmark (no video_id, no position, no state.lastWatched) sailed straight through and defaulted watchedAt to 'now', on every single poll cycle, forever - which is exactly why cleanup never stuck and the same titles kept reappearing with a fresh timestamp each time no matter how many times the DB was cleaned. Added the same isActuallyWatched gate (requires timeWatched, or progress >= 5% of runtime, or a video_id when duration is unavailable) directly in metricsProcessor.js before recordMovieWatch/recordEpisodeWatch run.

title: Show real avatars on Dashboard and Metrics pages, not just the Activity page
features: v1.9.6 added avatarUrl to the Activity page's user data, but three other surfaces build their user objects from separate code paths that were missed: the Metrics page's User Leaderboard and Watch Streaks panels (sourced from watchActivity.byUser), and the Dashboard's Recent Activity and Top Viewers widgets (the latter also sourced from watchActivity.byUser). All four now carry avatarUrl/useGravatar through from the database and pass it to UserAvatar, so a user's real avatar (e.g. a custom uploaded image) shows consistently across the whole app instead of only on the Activity page.

title: Stop preview/hover autoplay from counting as a real watch; show real avatars on Activity cards
features: Activity cards (both grid and list view) now show the actual user avatar (avatarUrl, respecting useGravatar) instead of always falling back to a generic letter badge. Previously the Activity feed's user objects never included avatarUrl, so UserAvatar had nothing to render but initials, even for accounts with a real avatar set (as seen on the Users page).
bugFixes: isActuallyWatched() still had a gap after v1.9.3: it checked state.overallTimeWatched > 0, but overallTimeWatched was being set to the same raw position value as timeOffset - so any nonzero position (including a few seconds of Nuvio's hover/preview-autoplay while just browsing the library) still counted as a full watch. The item's actual duration was never even captured from the API response to compare against. getLibrary() now records state.duration alongside timeOffset, and isActuallyWatched requires progress to be at least 5% of the runtime (same threshold used by AIOManager) instead of just nonzero - filters out preview noise while still catching real partial watches. Falls back to requiring a video_id when duration isn't available (e.g. live TV/IPTV items).

title: Label Activity feed items with which Nuvio profile they were watched under
features: Since v1.9.4 now syncs all Nuvio profiles instead of just the primary one, movie/episode watch entries can come from different profiles on the same account (e.g. a household's Main, Secondary, and Test profiles). Each entry is now tagged with the Nuvio profile name it came from and stored in a new profileLabel column on EpisodeWatchHistory and MovieWatchHistory. The Activity page (both grid and list views) shows this as a small badge on each card when known, so activity from different profiles stays visually distinguishable instead of being merged anonymously.

title: Sync watch activity across all Nuvio profiles, not just the primary one
bugFixes: getLibrary() in the Nuvio provider hardcoded p_profile_id: 1 on every RPC call (sync_pull_library, sync_pull_watch_progress, sync_pull_watched_items), so any watch activity happening under a second, third, etc. Nuvio profile was invisible to SlickSync no matter how long you waited - not a polling delay, the wrong profile was being queried, permanently. Confirmed via direct sync_pull_profiles/sync_pull_watch_progress queries: live playback was landing on a non-primary profile that was never being fetched.

getLibrary() now calls sync_pull_profiles first and fetches library/progress/watched-items for every profile the account has, merging the results (most-recent watch wins if the same title shows progress on more than one profile). Falls back to profile 1 alone if the profile list can't be fetched, so single-profile accounts are unaffected. getAddons()/setAddons() are intentionally left on profile 1 only for now - out of scope for this fix.

title: Fix false 'watched' activity for library items that were only saved, not played
bugFixes: isActuallyWatched() in both activityMonitor.js and metricsBuilder.js was treating any item with a non-empty state.video_id as watched. Nuvio creates a watch_progress row (with video_id populated, but position 0) the instant an item is bookmarked/saved to the library, not just when it's actually played - so movies added to your Saved library but never opened were being logged as real watch activity in the Activity feed, timestamped to when they were saved. Now requires state.timeOffset > 0 (genuine playback progress) alongside video_id before counting an item as watched.

title: Attempt fix for backend logs never appearing in docker logs
bugFixes: Confirmed via testing: the backend process (run via bun, backgrounded in start.sh) was producing zero visible output after its startup lines - not even its own "server running" log, despite the process functioning completely normally (serving requests successfully, passing health checks). The frontend, also backgrounded but run via a different process, logged normally the whole time. That strongly points to bun fully-buffering stdout/stderr instead of line-buffering it once backgrounded in a non-TTY context, rather than anything wrong with the application code itself - console output was very likely accumulating in an internal buffer that was rarely or never flushed.

Added stdbuf -oL -eL to force line-buffered output on the backend process, and added coreutils to the production image to provide it. Verified stdbuf correctly execs into the wrapped process rather than staying around as a separate parent, so the existing PID-based shutdown/cleanup logic is unaffected. Could not fully verify this resolves bun's specific buffering behavior without bun available to test directly - this is a well-reasoned, standard fix for this exact class of problem, but needs real confirmation after deploying.

title: Fix activity monitor silently swallowing errors
bugFixes: The scheduled 5-minute activity/metrics check had two bare catch blocks that logged nothing at all (one explicitly commented "Silently fail - dont spam logs"). Any genuine, persistent error in that cycle - a bad database query, a broken model reference, anything - was completely indistinguishable from "nothing new happened this cycle", with zero trace in the logs. Both now log a warning (and the per-account failure logs the full stack trace) so a real failure is visible instead of silent.

title: Fix Activity page and Watch Time Today showing no data
features: Added movie watch history tracking (a movie equivalent of the existing episode watch history), populated by the same reliable pipeline that already tracks episodes for both Stremio and Nuvio.
bugFixes: The Activity page's main feed, and the Dashboard's Recent Activity widget, were built entirely from WatchSession data - a heuristic that requires an item's progress to visibly change between two 5-minute polls to register at all, built specifically around Stremio's data shape and confirmed unreliable for both providers in this setup. Both now merge in a new reliable activity feed (built from watch history that a separate, already-proven pipeline populates correctly) for anything sessions missed, deduplicated against session entries so nothing shows twice.

"Watch Time Today" on the Activity page, and the Watch Time Trend graph on the Metrics page, were also reading from the same unreliable session data - already-correct totals computed from watch activity data earlier in the same request were being discarded in favor of the sparse session-based numbers. Both now use the reliable totals directly.

title: Fix ghost/deleted user still showing in Dashboard Recent Activity
bugFixes: The Dashboard's Recent Activity widget reads from the same WatchSession data as the old leaderboard did, and had the identical ghost-user bug: a deleted/recreated connection's leftover session rows still showed up with their raw database ID as a display name. Sessions belonging to users that no longer exist are now filtered out entirely instead of falling back to showing the raw ID, matching the same guard already used elsewhere in the metrics builder.

title: Fix Metrics leaderboard and summary showing wrong/missing data
bugFixes: The Metrics leaderboard and totals were built from WatchSession data (a hash-comparison heuristic that detects live watching by checking whether an item's progress changed between 5-minute polls) instead of the already-reliable WatchActivity table. In a confirmed real case, a user with 326 real WatchActivity rows had zero qualifying WatchSession rows and was completely missing from the leaderboard, while a deleted/orphaned user's leftover session rows still showed up with their raw database ID as a display name. The summary totals (movies/shows/watch time) were also being reset to 0 and recomputed from this same unreliable session data, discarding correct numbers that had already been computed from WatchActivity earlier in the same request.

Checked both the original upstream project and another Stremio manager for a working session-tracking reference for Nuvio specifically, and confirmed neither actually tracks it - this looks like a broader gap in how session-detection heuristics translate to Nuvio's data shape, not something specific to this fork. Rather than try to extend that heuristic, the leaderboard and summary now build from the same WatchActivity data that was already working correctly elsewhere on the Metrics and Activity pages.

title: Fix constant Nuvio token refresh spam and 429 rate limits
bugFixes: createProvider() is called fresh on every single operation (every activity check, every sync, every addon fetch) rather than being reused across a session. Without any caching, this forced a brand new Nuvio token refresh on every single one of those calls, even seconds apart, even though a Supabase access token is normally valid for about an hour. That is what caused the constant "Nuvio token refresh failed" log spam and 429 rate limit responses - added a module-level token cache (keyed by Nuvio user ID) so a still-valid token gets reused across separate provider instances instead of being refreshed every time.

Also fixed a related race condition: Supabase refresh tokens are single-use and rotate on every refresh, so two concurrent requests for the same user could have raced to refresh with the same (about to be invalidated) token, causing one of them to fail. Concurrent callers for the same user now share one in-flight refresh instead of firing duplicate requests.

title: Fix a server crash (undefined AUTH_ENABLED) and two more same-email-different-provider blocks
bugFixes: Fixed a server crash: AUTH_ENABLED was referenced in two places (the Stremio /connect endpoint, and the general user-update endpoint) but never actually defined anywhere in the codebase - a leftover from an old refactor that renamed this to INSTANCE_TYPE everywhere else. Replaced both with the same INSTANCE_TYPE check used consistently elsewhere.

While fixing the crash, found the same email-scoping bug from v1.8.4 in two more places: the Stremio /connect endpoint (logging into an existing Stremio account) and the general user-update endpoint (changing a user's email) both checked for ANY existing user with that email instead of scoping to the same provider type. Both now correctly allow a Stremio account and a Nuvio account to share an email.

title: Fix: could not have a Stremio and Nuvio account with the same email
bugFixes: Creating a Stremio user checked whether ANY user (regardless of provider) already used that email, instead of scoping the check to Stremio accounts specifically. This meant if a Nuvio account already existed with a given email, creating a Stremio account with that same email was silently blocked - the database itself has always correctly allowed the same email across both providers (a composite email+providerType constraint), this was purely an overly broad check in the Stremio creation endpoint. The equivalent Nuvio endpoint already scoped this correctly; the Stremio one now matches it.

title: Fix group avatars not showing on the Groups list page
bugFixes: The Groups list page (both table and card views) never passed the avatarUrl prop to the Avatar component at all, so custom group photos only ever showed on the group detail page, never in the list. Backend already returned the data correctly - this was purely a missing prop on the frontend.

title: Fix custom avatar images rendering huge, and fix drag-drop tab highlighting for real this time
bugFixes: Avatar images with a custom className (e.g. group avatars, which add a shadow/ring) lost their size constraint entirely instead of keeping it alongside the custom styling. With nothing capping the dimensions, a real uploaded image rendered at its full natural size instead of the intended small circle.

The v1.8.1 tab-alignment fix addressed a real bug but was not the whole story: the drag system used closestCenter collision detection everywhere, which compares the dragged card's own center to each target's center rather than the actual cursor position - fine for reordering similarly-sized cards, wrong for a card with a far-off center being dropped onto a small, distant tab or the sidebar link. Switched to a hybrid strategy: precise pointer-position detection first, falling back to closestCenter only for the gap-between-cards reordering case.

title: Fix Vault category tab drag-drop highlighting the wrong tab
bugFixes: Dragging a Vault entry over a category tab was highlighting a different tab than the one under the cursor, and could leave a tab stuck highlighted after ending the drag. Caused by an unstyled wrapper div added around each droppable tab that didn't match the button's own flex sizing, throwing off drag-and-drop hit detection. Fixed by giving the wrapper the same flex sizing the button used to have directly.

title: Vault: right-click menu, drag-to-recategorize, drag-to-Addons; bulk Move to Vault
features: Vault entries now also support right-click for a context menu (Run Check Now, Edit, Open Dashboard, Move to Addons, Delete) alongside the existing icon row
Drag any Vault entry onto a category tab to recategorize it on the spot
Drag any Vault entry onto "Addons" in the sidebar to move it there directly
Addons page: select one or more addons and use the new "Move to Vault" bulk action in the floating selection bar
miscChores: Reworked the Vault page drag system onto a single shared DndContext spanning the whole admin layout (not just the page), which is what makes dropping onto the sidebar possible

title: Move addons to/from Vault, drag-and-drop reordering
features: Addons page: new "Move to Vault" action per addon - creates a tracked Vault entry from the addon's manifest URL (with a live reachability check) and removes the addon from active management. If the addon was assigned to any group, it is removed from those too
Vault: new "Move to Addons" action per entry - reveals the stored secret, and if it looks like a manifest URL, recreates it as a real addon and removes the Vault entry
Vault entries can now be reordered by drag-and-drop within a single category (select a specific category tab, not All, to enable dragging)
bugFixes: Caught and fixed a route-ordering bug in the same commit that introduced it: the new /vault/reorder endpoint was registered after the existing /vault/:id handler, so Express matched "reorder" as an :id parameter and the reorder request never reached its real handler

title: Category-specific Vault forms with real login validation
features: Vault Add Entry form is now category-aware: Stremio/Nuvio categories ask for email+password and validate by actually logging in; Usenet Indexers ask for a Newznab URL + API key; Usenet Providers ask for host/port/username/password/SSL/data cap
Two new active-checks: stremio_auth and nuvio_auth, which log in for real rather than just pinging a server
bugFixes: Fixed a real bug where a single failed avatar image load could permanently hide all future valid images for that component instance (imageError state never reset when the src changed)
Added defensive inline max-width to the Modal component and console diagnostics on image load failure

title: Fix avatar bugs: uploaded images 404ing, list page not showing custom avatars, distorted sizing
bugFixes: Uploaded avatar images never actually displayed: they were served by the backend at /uploads/avatars/... but Next.js (the public-facing server) had no rule to proxy that path through to the backend, so every uploaded image silently 404d and fell back to the initial. Image URL mode worked around this by coincidence, since it points at an external absolute URL rather than this internal path.
The Users list page (both table and card views) fetched avatarUrl correctly but never passed it to the avatar component - custom avatars only appeared once you opened a user's detail page

Fixed a sizing conflict in the shared Avatar component: passing both a size preset and a custom className with different width/height created two independent, conflicting size sources (outer wrapper vs inner image container), which is what caused the distorted/stretched look on the user detail page header

title: Custom profile pictures for users and groups
features: Users and groups can now have a custom picture instead of the generated color/initial avatar
Set it via an image URL, or upload a file directly (JPEG/PNG/GIF/WEBP, 5MB limit)
Group cloning now carries over the avatar from the original group

title: README overhaul + proper credits
miscChores: Rewrote README to reflect the actual current feature set (Vault, badges, redesign, backup export)
Added a dedicated Credits section: iamneur0 (original SlickSync creator), Avangelista (Nuvio integration concepts), Sonicx161/AIOManager (Vault design inspiration)

title: Vault backup export (foundation for external backup + Bitwarden sync)
features: Vault entries now export to a decrypted JSON snapshot nightly, written to data/backup/vault/ on the server, ready to be pulled off-server for backup or synced into an external password manager
On-demand backup via POST /api/vault/backup-now instead of waiting for the nightly schedule

title: Fix Vault category tabs overlapping on mobile
bugFixes: Vault's category filter used equal-width flex tabs with no horizontal scroll, so on narrow phone screens all 10 category labels squeezed into tiny slots and wrapped/overlapped illegibly. Switched to the existing responsive tab component, which shows a clean dropdown on mobile and the normal tab row on desktop.

title: Provider badges + Vault category update
features: Stremio/Nuvio badge now shown next to every user — in the users list (table and card views) and on the user detail page
Vault categories: replaced Torrent Indexers and Subtitles with dedicated Stremio and Nuvio categories
bugFixes: providerType was already being fetched from the database in several places but was never actually included in the user list/detail API responses — fixed

title: Critical fixes: rate limiter crash, Nuvio auth root cause
bugFixes: Fixed a crash on every rate-limited API request (including Nuvio connect) caused by 'trust proxy' being set to trust all hops instead of exactly one (Traefik) — express-rate-limit refuses to run with that permissive a setting
Found and fixed the real cause of Nuvio login failures: the default backend URL pointed at a decommissioned raw Supabase project URL; corrected to Nuvio's actual current endpoint (api.nuvio.tv)
Nuvio auth errors now surface the real reason from Nuvio's servers instead of a generic 'Authentication failed' message

title: Visual redesign: new default theme, bolder look, nav fixes
features: New 'Slick' theme (bold violet + electric cyan) as the new default look, alongside all 7 original themes
Actually loaded the Space Grotesk / Outfit font pairing the design system was already configured for but never loading
Every card now has a gradient accent stripe; page titles render as gradient text; sidebar logo and panel redesigned with real depth/glow
Sidebar highlighting is now purely hover-driven — the bold colored pill only appears on real-time cursor hover, any item, with a subtle 'current page' marker instead of a permanent fill
bugFixes: Fixed a sidebar highlight that could get stuck lit after moving the mouse, caused by fragile JS-driven hover state — replaced with plain CSS :hover
Fixed a missing date-fns dependency that had been silently dropped somewhere along the way

title: Credential Vault + full SlickSync rebrand
features: New Vault: encrypted credential storage with expiry tracking, active-checks (Real-Debrid, TorBox, Newznab indexers, generic HTTP, TCP reachability), and ntfy/Discord notifications on expiry or failure
Complete rebrand from SlickSync to SlickSync across every backend and frontend string, log message, notification footer, and the Nuvio device name
miscChores: Renamed the project standalone from its upstream fork lineage

title: Nuvio provider support
features: Nuvio added as a first-class provider alongside Stremio — OAuth device-code (QR) and email/password login, in the Add User flow
Sync engine, watch-history metrics, user expiration, and library read/backup all made provider-aware end to end
Addon snapshots: save any user's or group's addon set as a reusable template and deploy it to anyone
Provider parity view and per-group activity dashboard (member roster, addon counts, real watch-time)
Anti-lockout encryption key: auto-generates and persists if unset, keeps the old key as a fallback if rotated
bugFixes: The general API rate limiter was defined but never actually mounted upstream — enabled it, plus stricter limits on credential/OAuth endpoints
